title: Modules, Packages, and Dependency Boundaries · Mastery Check
summary: Split the status coordinator into explicit browser modules and verify clean-environment loading and dependency behavior. Every item uses a fresh case and misconception-specific distractors. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a modular status application with a dependency record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use named, default, namespace, side-effect, and re-export forms with explicit public boundaries.
Trace static module graph linking, live bindings, execution order, and cyclic dependency risks.
Use dynamic import for conditional capability or measured loading needs with complete error states.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.javascript:
// javascript-basics cumulative stakeholder program.
// Add one runnable behavior, assertion, changed case, and evidence return per activity.

steps.title: Esm Import Export · changed case
steps.instruction:
quiz case — Split status parsing, policy, and UI adapters into ESM modules for release 2. Verify live binding behavior, package export boundaries, dependency provenance, and a clean import in a fresh runtime.

Which action demonstrates Esm Import Export without dropping earlier contracts?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for use named, default, namespace, side-effect, and re-export forms with explicit public boundaries.

Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: Default exports are required for one-value modules.

options.text: Declare inputs, observable output, ownership, and the runtime behavior being tested
Trace values, bindings, receivers, mutations, and queued work before editing code
Implement the smallest behavior and retain an assertion over the actual result
Run boundary and changed-event cases, inspect failures, and remove accidental coupling

steps.title: Defend one difficult decision

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Minimize, verify, update, and audit dependencies while treating install scripts and transitive code as executable supply-chain input.
